brief_document.title: Workflow list view — filter, paginate, query
brief_document.purpose: Give the operator the entry point of the dashboard: a filterable, paginated list of workflows backed by the server's query API, with the production states a real console needs.
brief_document.task: Build the workflow-list feature: a TanStack Query hook over the query API, a filter bar (type, status, time range), pagination against the server's contract, and a list of rows that link to the detail view — with explicit loading, empty, and error states.

requirements.title: Query hook and list rendering
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL load workflow summaries via TanStack Query against the query API and render them as rows showing id, type, status, and start/end time, each linking to the detail view.
acceptance: src/features/workflow-list/hooks/useWorkflowQuery.ts wraps the api client's queryWorkflows in a TanStack Query keyed by filter + page + namespace.
WorkflowList renders a WorkflowRow per WorkflowSummary showing id, type, a StatusBadge, and start/end time.
The hook returns query state (loading/error/data) for the view to render states from.
A render test with a mocked client shows rows for a returned page.

requirements.title: Filter bar and pagination
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a filter bar filtering by workflow type, status, and time range that drives the query, and pagination against the server's pagination contract that never loads full histories.
acceptance: FilterBar exposes type (text/select), status (WorkflowStatus select), and a time-range control; changing any updates the WorkflowFilter driving the query.
Pagination renders next/previous (or load-more) using the client's pagination contract and resets to the first page when the filter changes.
The list requests only summaries (query API), never per-workflow histories.
A test changing a filter asserts the query is re-issued with the new WorkflowFilter and pagination resets.

requirements.title: Loading, empty, and error states
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL render explicit loading (skeleton rows), empty (no workflows match this filter), and error (cause plus retry) states for the list, distinct from one another.
acceptance: While loading, the list shows layout-reserving skeleton rows (the shared LoadingSkeleton), not a bare spinner.
When the query returns no rows, the shared EmptyState renders 'no workflows match this filter'.
On query error, the shared ErrorState renders the cause and a retry affordance that re-runs the query.
A test asserts each of the three states renders for the corresponding query state.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add live row updates — that is AU-009 (this brief provides the query-driven list it patches).
SHALL NOT build the detail/timeline view — AU-005.
SHALL NOT define the namespace selector — AU-006 (this brief consumes the selected namespace in its query key).
SHALL NOT define the shared EmptyState/ErrorState/LoadingSkeleton/StatusBadge components — AU-008 (this brief consumes them).
verification: bun test passes for the list view tests.
No lint-disable comments added; the list never fetches full histories.

checklist.text: The list view loads workflow summaries via TanStack Query against the query API and renders them as rows (id, type, status, start/end time).
A filter bar filters by workflow type, status, and time range, driving the query.
The list paginates against the server's pagination contract and does not load full histories.
The list renders explicit loading (skeleton rows), empty (no workflows match this filter), and error (cause + retry) states.
Each row links to the workflow detail view.

stories.text: As an on-call operator, I want a filterable, paginated list of workflows by type, status, and time range so that I can quickly find what is running and what has failed without scrolling thousands of rows.
As an on-call operator, I want explicit empty and error states so that I can tell the difference between 'nothing matches' and 'the query failed', instead of staring at a spinner.

constraints.text: Match the apps/web stack exactly: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives, TanStack Query. No new framework, styling system, or state library.

Wire types come from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/). The dashboard never hand-defines a type aion-core/aion-proto owns and never hand-edits a generated file.

The dashboard consumes the AW API/WebSocket contract; it defines no server endpoint, no WebSocket protocol, and no asset-hosting mechanism. Not-yet-pinned contract details are isolated in the api module for a one-file change.
Feature-folder convention from apps/web: each feature is components/ + hooks/ (+ context/lib as needed) + types.ts + index.ts. Barrels re-export; logic lives in named files.
Server state goes through TanStack Query; live updates flow through the WebSocket manager and patch query caches or local view state. No bespoke fetch-into-useState for server data.
Every async surface renders explicit loading, empty, and error states. No surface spins indefinitely or blanks on failure.

The WebSocket manager reconnects with bounded backoff on drop and the active view resyncs (re-fetch history/query, or replay after last-seen sequence where supported). A connection indicator is always present; a dropped socket never leaves a silently stale view.
The event timeline is ordered strictly by event sequence number and de-duplicates events arriving via both history fetch and live stream.
Biome lint + format clean (bun run check) and tsc --noEmit clean. No lint-disable comments to silence findings.
Namespace selection scopes every query and every subscription; switching namespace re-scopes both. No unscoped query when a namespace is selected.

resolved_context.intention: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event history rendered as a timeline, and watch events stream live as they happen. This cluster builds the frontend only. It consumes the HTTP/gRPC list/query/history API and the WebSocket event stream that the server (cluster AW) exposes and hosts as static assets; it does not run the engine, persist anything, or define the API. It must match the existing house frontend stack (React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Biome + Bun, shadcn/Radix, TanStack Query, a singleton WebSocket manager with reconnect, generated types from Rust) exactly, so it reads as part of the same codebase as apps/web. When done, an operator pointed at a running aion-server can observe every workflow in every namespace, drill into any execution's full history, and watch events stream in real time — with production-grade loading, empty, error, and reconnection behaviour throughout.

provenance.quote: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event
